faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about PNG to WEBP conversion.
faq1Question: Is the PNG to WEBP converter free?
faq1Answer: Yes, completely free. No signup, no subscription, no hidden fees. Convert as many PNG files as you need.
faq2Question: Will converting to WEBP reduce image quality?
faq2Answer: No. Our converter uses lossless WEBP encoding, which preserves every pixel. You get a smaller file with identical visual quality.
faq3Question: Does WEBP support transparency like PNG?
faq3Answer: Yes. WEBP fully supports alpha transparency, so your transparent PNGs will remain transparent after conversion.
faq4Question: How much smaller will my files be?
faq4Answer: Typically 25–35% smaller than the original PNG. The exact reduction depends on image content, but WEBP consistently outperforms PNG in file size.
faq5Question: Is WEBP supported by all browsers?
faq5Answer: Yes. WEBP is supported by Chrome, Firefox, Safari (since 14), Edge, and all modern mobile browsers. It is now the standard format for web images.
seoSection1Title: What Is WEBP and Why Should You Use It?
seoSection1Para1: WEBP is a modern image format developed by Google, designed specifically for the web. It uses advanced compression algorithms to deliver smaller file sizes than PNG and JPEG while maintaining excellent visual quality.
seoSection1Para2: For website owners, switching from PNG to WEBP can significantly improve page load speed, reduce bandwidth usage, and boost Core Web Vitals scores — all factors that affect SEO rankings.
seoSection2Para: PNG is a lossless format ideal for graphics with transparency, while WEBP builds on those strengths with dramatically better compression. A PNG logo that weighs 150 KB can often be reduced to under 100 KB as WEBP — with zero visible quality difference.
seoSection3Title: When to Use WEBP on Your Website
seoSection3Para: Use WEBP for all images on your website — product photos, blog images, icons, and logos. Most modern CMS platforms like WordPress, Shopify, and Webflow support WEBP natively. Converting your existing PNG library to WEBP is one of the easiest performance wins available.
